content: Existing Self-Refine frameworks can be enhanced by integrating multiple inconsistent self-reflection perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms to unlock stronger unsupervised performance gains, creating a new layered self-improvement architecture that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no extra training or labeled data. This architecture lets LLMs catch blind spots in their own outputs that single-perspective iterative refinement would miss, by leveraging contradictions between different self-generated solving perspectives as a source of self-feedback.
